data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister: The current situation in the Strait of Hormuz is caused by the United States and Israel.
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated on the 22nd, according to Iranian media reports, that the current situation in the Strait of Hormuz is a direct result of the military strikes launched by the United States and Israel against a UN member state. The report said that Araqchi spoke by phone with Italian Foreign Minister Tajani, exchanging views on regional developments and the security, legal, and economic impacts of the US-Israeli military strikes against Iran. Araqchi said that Iran, as a littoral state of the Strait of Hormuz, has taken measures in accordance with international law to safeguard its national security from US and Israeli aggression and threats. He emphasized that the consequences for the global economy should be borne by the aggressors. Araqchi also stated that the US-Israeli strikes against Iran's peaceful nuclear facilities are unacceptable, and the silence of some European countries on such actions is a "double standard" that is undermining international law and non-proliferation mechanisms. Tajani emphasized that achieving peace and stability in the Persian Gulf region is crucial, and Italy is willing to play a positive role in promoting overall regional stability. (Xinhua)

data.title: BNB Surpasses 650 USDT with a 3.39% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 22, 2026, 13:42 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 650 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 653.109985 USDT, with a narrowed 3.39% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Aethir Launches AI Agent Hosting Platform with V1 Release
data.description:
Aethir has announced the release of version 1 of its AI agent hosting platform, Aethir Claw. According to Odaily, this new version allows users to deploy and configure AI agents entirely within a browser, without requiring any technical background, in a process that takes less than five minutes.

The V1 release includes a pre-built general AI assistant role with over 50 skills that can be integrated directly into user workflows. The platform natively supports payments in cryptocurrencies such as ATH, USDC, and USDT. It employs a fully isolated container architecture for security, allowing users to hold their SSH keys and enabling the platform to be set to zero access, meeting enterprise-level security standards.

Aethir also previewed a specialized AI agent for crypto trading users, known as C.A.R.A, which will offer market monitoring and on-chain sentiment analysis capabilities.

The entire platform operates on Aethir's decentralized GPU cloud infrastructure, with each deployment instance independently isolated, eliminating reliance on traditional centralized cloud service providers.

In the future, Aethir plans to introduce Model as a Service (MaaS) integration, allowing users to access open-source large language models directly on the platform without needing to configure third-party API keys, providing a comprehensive solution with a single payment.

data.title: Britain Faces Economic Challenges Amid Iran Conflict and Inflation Concerns
data.description: Britain began the year with optimism for an economic recovery. Bloomberg posted on X, however, the onset of the Iran war has shifted favorable conditions into challenges. The primary concern now is whether the nation will be more affected by rising inflation or decelerating growth.

data.title: Anubis Chain launches Ecosystem Co-creation Program: Superman
data.description: Anubis Chain officially announced the launch of the "Superhero for All" program, recruiting ecosystem co-creation participants globally through an open collaboration mechanism. The program revolves around three career paths: Brand Promotion Officer, Community Co-creation Officer, and Ecosystem Alliance Officer. Participants will receive platform token airdrop incentives and ecosystem benefits, further promoting the globalization of the Anubis Chain privacy infrastructure network.

data.title: Apple's Ecosystem Faces Security Concerns Amid Executive Change
data.description: Apple announced that John Ternus will assume the role of chief executive officer by September 1. According to NS3.AI, Kaspersky Threat Research has identified at least 26 fraudulent cryptocurrency wallet applications within Apple's ecosystem. The campaign has been attributed to SparkKitty, which has reportedly been active since late 2025. Earlier this month, musician G. Love reported losing 5.9 Bitcoin, valued at approximately $436,000, after downloading what he thought was a legitimate Ledger app.

data.title: Aethir Claw V1 is officially launched: Deploy encrypted native AI agents in five minutes with no code required.
data.description: Aethir's AI agent hosting platform, Aethir Claw, today released version V1. The core breakthrough of this version is that users can complete the deployment and configuration of the AI ​​agent entirely within a browser, requiring no technical background—the official process is claimed to take no more than five minutes. Version V1 comes pre-built with a general-purpose AI assistant role, featuring over 50 built-in skills, and can be directly integrated into user workflows. The platform natively supports cryptocurrency payments such as ATH, USDC, and USDT. For security, it employs a fully isolated container architecture, with users holding their own SSH keys, allowing the platform to set access permissions to zero, achieving enterprise-grade security standards. The dedicated agent C.A.R.A. for crypto traders was also announced, offering market monitoring and on-chain sentiment analysis capabilities, and will be available soon. The entire platform runs on Aethir's own decentralized GPU cloud infrastructure, with each deployment instance independently isolated, without relying on traditional centralized cloud service providers. In the future, Aethir will also launch MaaS (Model as a Service) integration, at which time users will be able to directly call open-source large language models within the platform without configuring any third-party API keys, achieving one-time payment and full-stack coverage.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson Increases Quarterly Dividend Amid Legal Challenges
data.description: Johnson & Johnson has announced a 3.1% increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it from $1.30 to $1.34. This marks the company's 64th consecutive dividend hike, with the payment scheduled for June 9, 2026. According to NS3.AI, the company continues to face pressure from ongoing talc litigation, as a federal judge in Texas recently rejected its latest settlement proposal.

data.title: New York Post: Trump says talks with Iran could happen as early as Friday
data.description: The New York Post reports that Trump said he could hold talks with Iran as early as Friday.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Indicates Possible Start of Iran Talks This Friday
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested that negotiations with Iran "might" commence this Friday. According to BlockBeats, this information was reported by the New York Post, citing a text message from President Trump.

data.title: Precious metals and crude oil rose across the board, while BTC/ETH volatility narrowed.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $4,747.30 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.57%. Silver prices rose to $77.884 per ounce, a daily increase of 1.58%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 43.50, a daily decrease of 1.49%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 64.50, a daily decrease of 3.43%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate remained unchanged at 6.82657. The USD/JPY exchange rate fell 0.09% to 159.249. In commodities, WTI crude oil rose 0.72% to $94.46 per barrel. Brent crude oil rose 0.56% to $100.86 per barrel. Gate allows users to trade traditional financial market products directly within the platform, providing a one-stop solution for various asset classes including precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration between crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi functionality is fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to conveniently participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities beyond the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Desay SV Develops Smart Driving Solution with NVIDIA Technology
data.description: Desay SV is developing a mass-production smart driving solution based on NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion's NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Thor accelerated computing platform. According to Jin10, the solution incorporates NVIDIA NVLink interconnect technology, making Desay SV one of the first partners to apply this technology. Additionally, this technology can be extended to embodied intelligence and other edge computing fields.

data.title: GSR Launches First Multi-Asset Crypto ETF on Nasdaq
data.description:
Cryptocurrency market maker GSR has introduced its first multi-asset crypto ETF, named GSR Crypto Core3 ETF, with the ticker BESO, now listed on Nasdaq. According to ChainCatcher, the ETF includes investments in Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and Solana (SOL), and incorporates staking yields where applicable.

The fund employs an active management strategy with plans to adjust its holdings weekly, and carries a management fee of 1%. The report highlights that this product is the first actively managed multi-asset crypto ETF in the United States to offer staking capabilities. This launch marks GSR's latest effort to expand its crypto ETF and asset management business.

data.title: WalletConnect Expands to Canton Network, Enhancing Institutional Finance Access
data.description: WalletConnect has integrated with the Canton Network, expanding its ecosystem of 55.5 million users to include access to the institutional finance blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this integration connects over 700 wallets and 70,000 decentralized applications, focusing on stablecoin payments, tokenized real-world assets, and decentralized finance (DeFi) use cases. Canton Network reportedly processes over $8 trillion in tokenized assets each month and settles more than $350 billion in U.S. Treasuries daily.

data.title: Polygon Labs Head Discusses Bridge Operations Post-KelpDAO Exploit
data.description: Polygon Labs Head of Product John Egan highlighted the resilience of Agglayer's proof-based bridge design following the KelpDAO exploit, which resulted in the draining of $292 million of rsETH. According to NS3.AI, Egan noted that despite the hack, Polygon PoS and Agglayer bridges continued to process approximately $200 million. Meanwhile, Aave experienced significant activity with $6.6 billion in withdrawals, and several protocols opted to pause related markets or bridges in response to the incident.

data.title: Sources: Trump is willing to give Iran another 3 to 5 days of ceasefire.
data.description: Axios, a US news website, reported on the 22nd, citing a US source, that US President Trump was willing to give Iran another 3 to 5 days of ceasefire, but "this will not be indefinite." (Xinhua)

data.title: European Commission Introduces New Energy Package Ahead of Cyprus Summit
data.description: The European Commission has introduced a new energy package as European Union leaders prepare for a summit in Cyprus scheduled for Thursday and Friday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this development in the context of ongoing energy challenges faced by the EU. The package aims to address energy security and sustainability, focusing on reducing dependency on external energy sources and promoting renewable energy initiatives. This move comes amid heightened concerns over energy supply disruptions and the need for a cohesive strategy to tackle climate change. The summit in Cyprus will provide a platform for EU leaders to discuss and potentially endorse the proposed measures, which are seen as crucial for the bloc's energy future.

data.title: Documentary Searches for Satoshi Nakamoto: Hal Finney and Len Sassaman May Be Co-Creators of Bitcoin
data.description: The documentary *Finding Satoshi*, released Wednesday, claims that Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto is not a single person, but an alias used by cryptographers Hal Finney and Len Sassaman. Directors Tucker Tooley and Matthew Miele, through a four-year investigation, indicate that Hal Finney was responsible for writing the Bitcoin code, while Len Sassaman was responsible for writing the written content, including the white paper. Hal Finney's widow, Fran Finney, admitted in an interview that her husband may have been involved in the creation of Bitcoin. Investigators used elimination methods to rule out other candidates, such as Adam Back, noting that Hal Finney and Len Sassaman's online activity records closely matched those of Satoshi Nakamoto. Additionally, the film included a 90-minute interview with Sam Bankman-Fried, but it was ultimately not used.

data.title: Kelp DAO Loses $292 Million in Hack Linked to North Korea's Lazarus Group
data.description:
Kelp DAO suffered a significant loss of $292 million due to a hack on Saturday. According to NS3.AI, preliminary findings by LayerZero suggest that the attack was carried out by TraderTraitor, a subgroup of North Korea's Lazarus Group. Kelp DAO attributed the exploit to a failure in LayerZero's infrastructure. LayerZero explained that Kelp DAO had used a single verifier configuration to approve cross-chain messages, which may have contributed to the vulnerability.

This incident, combined with the $285 million Drift exploit, has brought the total suspected North Korea-linked crypto theft to at least $578 million across major incidents in April.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Condemns Ongoing Maritime Blockade
data.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson has criticized the continued maritime blockade on Iranian ports, labeling it as an aggressive action. According to Odaily, the spokesperson emphasized the negative impact of the blockade on Iran's economy and international trade. The blockade has been a point of contention, affecting the country's ability to engage in global commerce effectively.
